codeReview.summary: The fix correctly switched sequence sourcing to runtime completion order, but two contract-critical blockers remain: candidate scoping still admits prerequisite/internal nodes after DAG completion, and winner-reason attribution is not strictly winner-vs-runner-up for success-over-failure.

issues.description: Candidate scoping is still incorrect. `findTerminalConflictingOutcomes()` accepts any completed/failed node whose dependents are terminal (`dependents.allSatisfy(...)`). Because winner selection is only invoked after `allTerminal` is true (line 311), this condition is satisfied for completed prerequisite/internal nodes as well, so non-terminal outcomes can still enter reconciliation candidates.

Winner-reason attribution is not fully runner-up delta based. The success check uses `sortedCandidates.dropFirst().contains` to detect any failed candidate, so with 3+ candidates it can emit `SUCCESS_OVER_FAILURE` even when the runner-up is successful. This violates deterministic winner-vs-runner-up attribution intent for persisted reason codes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The runtime-orchestration skill requires three focused daemon test commands (RunService, MissionControlService, ProviderRouter), but this fix handoff marked `followedProcedure: true` while running only MissionControl plus build/core tests.

summary: Fail. Runtime completion-sequence sourcing was improved, but the fix does not fully resolve the prior blocking scope/attribution concerns: internal nodes can still be candidate winners, and success-over-failure reasoning is not restricted to winner-vs-runner-up comparison.
